flavorText: The leaves on its head grow right back even if they fall out. These bitter leaves refresh those who eat them.
The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don’t groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.

attacks.text: This attack does 70 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It nocks its arrow quills and shoots them at opponents. When it simply can’t afford to miss, it tugs the vine on its head to improve its focus.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.
This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A life-form that lives in another world, its body is thin and supple, but it also possesses great power.
This Ultra Beast’s body, which is as thin as paper, is like a sharpened sword.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

flavorText: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.
If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.
It won’t attack while it’s alone—not even if it spots prey. Instead, it waits for other Carvanha to join it, and then the Pokémon attack as a group.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: As soon as it catches the scent of prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at 75 mph.
The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.
Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon do −20 damage.

flavorText: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.
When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.
When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.
As Emolga flutters through the air, it crackles with electricity. This Pokémon is cute, but it can cause a lot of trouble.

attacks.text: Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

abilities.text: At the end of your first turn, take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they’ll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.
It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.
A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.
With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.
People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it’s kind.
It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may put a random Pokémon from your deck into your hand.

flavorText: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.
Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.
It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

abilities.text: Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't be healed.

flavorText: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.
This Pokémon is very friendly when it’s young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.

attacks.text: Discard a {F} Energy from this Pokémon.
If Passimian is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon battles by throwing hard berries. It won’t obey a Trainer who throws Poké Balls without skill.
If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else—like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body. Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.

attacks.text: Discard a random Pokémon Tool card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it’s very picky about food.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The round face of Alolan Persian is considered to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region, so these Pokémon are very well cared for.
It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.
Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, a card is chosen at random from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon takes +10 damage from being Poisoned.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.

attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The metal-rich geology of this Pokémon’s habitat caused it to develop steel whiskers on its head.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It eats iron ore—and sometimes railroad tracks— to build up the steel armor that protects its body.
It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

flavorText: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.
It absorbs the iron it finds in the rock while clinging to the ceiling. It shoots spikes when in danger.

abilities.text: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, do 20 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon scrapes its spikes across rocks, and then uses the tips of its feelers to absorb the nutrients it finds within the stone.

attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.

flavorText: It appeared from an Ultra Wormhole. Each one appears to be made up of many life-forms stacked one on top of each other.
This Pokémon is courageous but also cautious. It uses the soft fur covering its face to collect information about its surroundings.
Herdier is a very smart and friendly Pokémon. So much so that there’s a theory that Herdier was the first Pokémon to partner with people.

abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent's Active Pokémon cost 1 {C} more.

flavorText: These Pokémon seem to enjoy living with humans. Even a Stoutland caught in the wild will warm up to people in about three days.
Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.

attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.
It normally spends its time meditating in the treetops. It throws Poké Balls and gives other Pokémon orders as it pleases.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon weapon developed for a specific mission, it went berserk during an experiment, so it was cryogenically frozen.

attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: This is its form once it has awakened and evolved. Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Ultra Beast with 1 of your Benched Ultra Beasts.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: One of the dangerous UBs, high energy readings can be detected coming from both of its huge arms.

trainer.text:
You can use this card only if your opponent hasn't gotten any points.

During your opponent's next turn, all of your Ultra Beasts take −20 damage from attacks from your opponent's Pokémon.
Switch out your opponent's Active Basic Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

If the {L} Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, move 2 {L} Energy from that Pokémon and attach 1 Energy each to 2 of your Benched Pokémon.
Attacks used by the Ultra Beast this card is attached to do +10 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each point you have gotten.
Put 1 random Type: Null or Silvally from your deck into your hand.
Your opponent reveals all of the Supporter cards in their deck.

You can use this card only if your opponent has gotten at least 1 point.

Choose 1 of your Ultra Beasts. Attach 2 random Energy from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It sends its feathers, which are as sharp as blades, flying in attack. Its legs are strong, so its kicks are also formidable.

attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A life-form that lives in another world, its body is thin and supple, but it also possesses great power.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 70 damage to itself.

flavorText: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It’s apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

attacks.text: Discard a random Pokémon Tool card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It’s accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it’s very picky about food.

attacks.text: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: This is its form once it has awakened and evolved. Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Ultra Beast with 1 of your Benched Ultra Beasts.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: One of the dangerous UBs, high energy readings can be detected coming from both of its huge arms.

attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.
Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Discard a {F} Energy from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.

trainer.text: Put 1 random Type: Null or Silvally from your deck into your hand.
Your opponent reveals all of the Supporter cards in their deck.

You can use this card only if your opponent has gotten at least 1 point.

Choose 1 of your Ultra Beasts. Attach 2 random Energy from your discard pile to that Pokémon.

attacks.text: Take a {L} Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Discard a {F} Energy from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.

flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.
It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.
It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise  control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may do 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water.  When it spins them and throws them at high speed,  these stars can split metal in two.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its strange cries sound like human language. There are some musicians who compose songs for Jynx to sing.
A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.
The claws on its feet are well developed. It can carry prey such as an Exeggcute to its nest over 60 miles away.

abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

flavorText: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as Magikarp.

attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon back into their deck.

flavorText: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.

attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.

abilities.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Active Pokémon.

attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

abilities.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon takes +10 damage from being Poisoned.

attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: A life-form from another world, it was dubbed a UB and is thought to produce a strong neurotoxin.
